acceptance_targets: [CH01_CRAFT_RITUAL_MECHANICS_BREAK] By reading the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, a cold reader can state what changed versus Maren's previous sham rituals, what the contact is acting through, and why this event is an exception instead of proof that every earlier fake ritual should also have worked.

[CH01_CRAFT_BOUNDARY_HANDOFF_MISMATCH] After revision, the widow, son, corpse, and farmhouse all carry the same surname on-page, and a reader can move from the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 into the provided boundary excerpt without inventing a missing exchange or reversing the order of the bowl return, the barred door, and Maren's walk home.

[CH01_CRAFT_INTERFACE_GUILT_LOOP] After Haff's cause of death lands, every remaining major exchange in the revised back half must change at least one on-page variable: operational knowledge, immediate plan, or relationship state. If a reader can paraphrase two later passages with the same sentence 'Aldric explains again that Maren's fraud harmed people,' the revision fails.

findings.acceptance_test: By reading the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, a cold reader can state what changed versus Maren's previous sham rituals, what the contact is acting through, and why this event is an exception instead of proof that every earlier fake ritual should also have worked.
findings.problem: The chapter explicitly frames Maren's ritual as invented posture plus nonsense murmuring and reminds us that nothing has ever happened during eleven years of false work, then gives him a real fragment acquisition anyway. Against the continuity-sheet world rule that fraudulent sin-eating leaves contamination and fragments intact unless the ritual is genuinely opened, this reads as a causality break rather than a managed mystery. The inciting event needs a local exception on the page, not just later explanation.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, add a concrete exceptional trigger that distinguishes this beat from Maren's prior fake rituals: Aldric reaching for him, Maren accidentally making one genuine opening, an abnormal contamination surge at Haff's table, or another specific mechanism. Keep the deeper chapter-12 truth back if needed, but make the immediate process legible enough that the reader understands why this false rite suddenly produces a real embedding.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, the widow, son, corpse, and farmhouse all carry the same surname on-page, and a reader can move from the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 into the provided boundary excerpt without inventing a missing exchange or reversing the order of the bowl return, the barred door, and Maren's walk home.
findings.problem: The chapter names the household Dunnock and stages Orla's silent bowl return plus the barred door as the aftermath beat, but the provided boundary continuation expects the same immediate aftermath with a different family name and a different exit sequence. As written, the chapter does not hand off cleanly into the next-chapter boundary material; one side of the continuity is wrong.
findings.rewrite_direction: Starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:11, choose one family identity and carry it consistently through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123. Then reconcile the exit order in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:301, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 so the bowl return, any final reassurance, the door bar, and Maren's departure occur in one unambiguous sequence that the next chapter can inherit without contradiction.

findings.acceptance_test: After Haff's cause of death lands, every remaining major exchange in the revised back half must change at least one on-page variable: operational knowledge, immediate plan, or relationship state. If a reader can paraphrase two later passages with the same sentence 'Aldric explains again that Maren's fraud harmed people,' the revision fails.
findings.problem: Once Aldric learns the fraud and then confirms that Haff died of accumulated contamination, the back half keeps rerunning the same moral diagnosis through additional interface readouts and accusation passes. The chapter does gain texture, but too many later beats certify guilt the reader already understands instead of creating a fresh decision, proof, or irreversible consequence, so the second half starts to feel like repetition-by-escalated-wording.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or repurpose the later readout blocks at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:203, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:291 so each surviving exchange adds a distinct turn: a new operational rule, a concrete next-step demand, a changed Maren/Aldric relationship, or an external consequence. Cut any pass whose only function is to restate that Maren lied and contamination harmed villagers.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
